message.content:
Let me analyze the task: 1. I need to create webpage 3 (site/page-3.html) 2. Link it back to page 2 3. Preserve existing page 1 and page 2 links Looking at the existing files: - site/index.html has a link to page-2.html - site/page-2.html has a link to index.html (Home) So the current chain is: - index.html -> page-2.html - page-2.html -> index.html For stage 3, I need to: 1. Create site/page-3.html 2. Add a link from page-3.html back to page-2.html 3. Possibly update page-2.html to link to page-3.html (to create a proper chain) 4. Keep the existing links intact The validators: - page-3-links-page-2: failure_class=link_integrity - page-3 must link to page-2 - previous-links-preserved: failure_class=link_integrity - existing links must be preserved So I need to: 1. Create site/page-3.html with a link back to page-2.html 2. Update site/page-2.html to also link to page-3.html (so users can navigate from page 2 to page 3) 3. Keep all existing links working Let me create the files: page-3.html: - Should have a nav with link to page-2.html - Should have main content page-2.html update: - Add a link to page-3.html in the nav Let me construct the JSON response. { "files": [ { "path": "site/page-3.html", "content": "\n\n\n \n \n Page 3\n\n\n
\n Page 2\n
\n
\n

Page 3

\n

This is the third page.

\n
\n\n" }, { "path": "site/page-2.html", "content": "\n\n\n \n \n Page 2\n\n\n
\n Home\n Page 3\n
\n
\n

Page 2

\n

This is the second page.

\n
\n\n" } ], "notes": "Created page-3.html with link to page-2.html, and updated page-2.html to link to page-3.html while preserving existing links." }
